Silver Dagger of Destruction  
 

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: RANGE PRIMARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 20
DMG: 19 Dmg Bonus: 35 AC: 50
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.
STR: +25 INT: +15 MANA: +50
WT: 0.0 Size: TINY
Class: NEC WIZ MAG ENC
Race: ALL
Slot 1, Type 4 (Weapon: General)
Slot 2, Type 20 (Ornamentation)

Lore Item No Trade Placeable
Slot: RANGE PRIMARY
Skill: 1H Piercing Atk Delay: 20
DMG: 19 Dmg Bonus: 35 AC: 50
This item is placeable in yards, guild yards, houses and guild halls.

WT: 0.0 Size: TINY

Item Updated By:SwiftyMUSE
Source:Live
IC Last Updated:2021-12-25 10:22:48
Page Updated:Thu Oct 9th, 2008

Expansion: Scars of Velious
Rarity: Very Rare
Level to Attain: 58
[Drops | Comments ]

This item is found on creatures.

NPC Name

A sad story
QuoteReply
# Oct 23 2001 at 6:15 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591770336824
19 posts
I think this a great addition for casters. I am a 45 enchanter on Xev and one day in WC a bloody puma jumped me and I couldn't kill it. I was level 45 and couldn't kill a level ten monster. (I didn't have mez or a DD memed at the time)

Would at least be nice to have a reason to work on your melee skills too, we get them so we should use them. Same goes for the girdle of rapidity. These items are for casters only because there would be no way for us to get haste gear and nice weapons if we had to compete with melee classes. Its a great addition and I look forward to getting one for myself =)

Now with velious out there is a haste robe out for casters lookin in to gettin one has haste and int and mana and AC what more could a caster need?

__DEL__1591761796382

uhhhh does Verant WANT casters to tank? I mean my chanter could frickin melee mobs to death with this thing, not to mention the handy +15 int. Myth And Legends

▲ Page top
RE: VI=BACKWARDS
QuoteReply
# Nov 10 2001 at 4:59 AM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1592247593307
15 posts
Dude, you would NEVER out-aggro a group of tanks with this weapon just meleeing. If it proc'ed a DD or a debuff, maybe. When I had this dagger in my primary the only reason the tanks didn't like me using it is because I got in the way. I missed A LOT, and when I hit it wasn't for very much, average teens I would say.

A caster's innate melee ability is complete crap, which is why they can afford to give us such great weapons. What this dagger is really GREAT for its stats, mainly the AC, mana, and str. Int is a negligible benefit unless possibly you are an iksar, or do a lot of trade skills.

This things a flippin sheild in itself and makes a great range slot item if you have another decent weapon or utility item in your primary slot. The dmg/delay is really only good for small crap you don't want to waste mana on and pvp, especially against another caster.

▲ Page top
Would it be good for a rogue if possible?
QuoteReply
# Oct 19 2001 at 3:38 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591769051269
74 posts
The thought of this being a rogue weapon is almost devious. The unimaginable damage a rogue could deal with this bad boy isnt to be thought about. Im a warrior and its often times my job to keep the rogues alive, cause they might hit hard, but they also GET HIT hard too. If you are a warrior you know the extreme difficulty it is to regain the taunt from a rogue with epic or better pokers. This thing would out taunt a warrior even without BS's. A rogue with the same weapons as me naturally does almost twice as much damage, then to add in BS's. And the extra 30 points in taunt is nice... buts its a far cry still in whats truelly needed for us warriors to regain taunt. The only thing that scares me more than these great weapons emerging, is the thought of even better armor for rogues. Id hate to think that the days of a warrior taking the beating, are numbered. But 2 rogues on opposite sides of a mob will slaugher the mob a hell of a lot faster than a tank on one, and a rogue on the other. Plus with good armor, both rogues are capapble of staying alive long enough with a cleric as to not need us tanks :( Ah well, we still got our good looks ;) oh yeah. Ladies take a number please, line forms to the left hehe

QuoteReply
# Oct 18 2001 at 1:37 PM Rating: Excellent
__DEL__1593966011035
4 posts
Okay, how is anyone even arguing that this is not one of the best daggers in the game? It's got almost a 1 to 1 ratio and an AC of 50!!!! Holy fricking banshee mother. Anyone who gets one of these should feel blessed beyond their wildest dreams, and anyone who sits on these message boards and analyzes stats and complains about Verant needs to just chill out and try to enjoy the game. It's one of the most fun games in the world, and this is a great item.

▲ Page top
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 18 2001 at 12:39 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) I'm going to ignore cratequing your poor writing skills and point out to you that hybrids and all classes for that matter had their experience penalties removed via a patch quite a while ago.
#REDACTED, Posted: Oct 18 2001 at 4:06 AM, Rating: Sub-Default, (Expand Post) Obviously you miss the little FACT that hybrid STILL needs more XP to gain levels then any other "pure" class. Why don't you wiseguy go back to the same patch history, check it all out again and also check the XP amounts needed for classes to level, and then start pointing the fingers. And as for your writing, frankly I don't understand what the heck were you trying to say there at the end. Don't cuss on other's writing if you cannot write yourself. Bonehead.
RE: Gm's are the devil
QuoteReply
# Oct 18 2001 at 9:09 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1593966052645
8 posts
Technically, you are correct - Hybrids do require more experience to level. However, they still level at the same rate as a druid, cleric, or shaman of the same race. The idea is that they originally had a modifier for exp required per level. If Verant removes that modifier, then a level 54 SK will suddenly become a level 57 SK. Instead, they added a bonus to all xp for classes who had a modifier worse than a cleric, druid, or shaman of the same race.

Bottom line? A SK Troll requires more xp to level than a Shaman Troll, but will level at the exact same rate.
▲ Page top
RE: Gm's are the devil
QuoteReply
# Oct 18 2001 at 9:07 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592757636566
17 posts
Well, in order to stop a flame, I must attempt to correct you all. Hybrid's do require more actual experience points than "normal" classes. It also happens that they get "free" experience each kill based on their original penalties. This has the secondary effect that if there is an experience bonus in the zone, that hybrids will even get a slightly better bonus than other classes. All of this has been stated by the developers.
▲ Page top
RE: Gm's are the devil
QuoteReply
# Oct 19 2001 at 6:15 AM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1591750438546
67 posts
Umm... I think you misunderstood the XP change that was made. The net effect is to almost totally ELIMINATE Class-based XP penalties (Warriors and Rogues get a Bonus and continue to do so) -- and to move the Racial penalty to one that only affects the "expensive race" character. For example, a Human paladin now levels at the same rate as a Human cleric -- if they always group together, they will always ding together. If however they are also with a Troll shaman, the troll will need 20% more exp, and will (very slowly) fall behind. This is how people assumed the experience penalties worked when EQ launched, but it wasn't made so until about 6 months ago.
▲ Page top
RE: Gm's are the devil
QuoteReply
# Oct 19 2001 at 2:14 PM Rating: Default
__DEL__1624235044287
192 posts
Ancaglon, I'm not sure exactly how it worked, but there is no effective penalty based on class anymore. Hybrids do still take more exp to level, but they get more exp each kill so it evens out. They did this because if they just changed the exp level requirements, you would suddenly see level 54 SK's jumping to level 57 like has been pointed out by other people. Those numbers still stay in effect because they're lazy and there's no real need to change it.

That's, at least, what I understand. Please don't flame me, just correct me if I'm wrong.
▲ Page top
RE: Gm's are the devil
QuoteReply
# Oct 18 2001 at 10:04 PM Rating: Decent
__DEL__1592249495182
101 posts
hmm well it would seem that they took my original post down because someone couldn't handle my kidding around.... but thanks for that info guys! had no idea that hybrids got that kind of exp bonus... hmmm now i will go and ponder that as i also ponder why they voted my post off
